tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and brown, cloudy urine. These findings suggest impaired renal function (possible acute or chronic kidney disease) and abnormal urinary findings, possibly due to nephrotic syndrome, tubular dysfunction, or severe metabolic disturbance. The low HbA1c with glucosuria and ketonuria may indicate recent-onset or poorly controlled diabetes or another metabolic disorder. Further clinical correlation and nephrology evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
